mission.title: Install and Configure Rancher / RKE2
mission.description: Rancher is a complete container management platform that simplifies the deployment and management of Kubernetes clusters across various environments. It is ideal for organizations looking to streamline their container orchestration and management processes. Key trade-offs include the need for adequate resources and potential complexity in multi-cluster management.

steps.title: Install the Rancher CLI
steps.description:
This step installs the Rancher CLI, which is essential for managing Rancher from the command line.

```bash

curl -LO https://github.com/rancher/cli/releases/download/v2.14.0/rancher-darwin-amd64-v2.14.0.tar.gz

tar -xzf rancher-darwin-amd64-v2.14.0.tar.gz

sudo mv rancher-darwin-amd64 /usr/local/bin/rancher

```

> Download the Rancher CLI for macOS.
> Extract the downloaded tarball.
> Move the CLI to a directory in your PATH.

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
This step creates a new Kubernetes cluster using RKE2 with the specified version and node count.

```bash

curl -sfL https://get.rke2.io | INSTALL_RKE2_VERSION=v1.30.0 sh -

sudo systemctl enable rke2-server.service

sudo systemctl start rke2-server.service

```

> Install RKE2 version 1.30.0.
> Enable the RKE2 server service.
> Start the RKE2 server.

steps.title: Set up kubeconfig
steps.description:
This step configures the kubeconfig file to allow kubectl to communicate with the new cluster.

```bash

sudo cp /etc/rancher/rke2/rke2.yaml ~/.kube/config

sudo chown $(id -u):$(id -g) ~/.kube/config

```

> Copy the RKE2 kubeconfig to the default location.
> Change ownership of the kubeconfig file.

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
This step verifies that the nodes in the cluster are up and running.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

kubectl get pods --all-namespaces

```

> List all nodes in the cluster to ensure they are ready.
> Check that all system pods are running.

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
This step includes optional configurations for autoscaling and monitoring.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/rancher/rancher/master/rke2/manifests/rancher-monitoring.yaml

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/rancher/rancher/master/rke2/manifests/rancher-autoscaler.yaml

```

> Install Rancher monitoring.
> Install Rancher autoscaler.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Rancher
uninstall.description:
This step removes Rancher and its associated resources. Be aware that this will result in data loss and orphaned resources.

```bash

sudo systemctl stop rke2-server.service

sudo rm -rf /var/lib/rancher

```

> Stop the RKE2 server.
> Remove Rancher data.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Rancher
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up your data and verify the current state of the cluster.

```bash

kubectl get all --all-namespaces > backup-$(date +%F).yaml

curl -sfL https://get.rke2.io | INSTALL_RKE2_VERSION=v1.31.0 sh -

kubectl get nodes

```

> Backup current state of all resources.
> Upgrade to RKE2 version 1.31.0.
> Verify that nodes are in a ready state after the upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Unable to connect to the server: x509: certificate signed by unknown authority'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates an issue with the kubeconfig file or the cluster's certificate.

**Fix:**
Ensure the kubeconfig file is correctly set up and points to the right cluster certificate.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to pull image'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This can occur if the image repository is unreachable or the image does not exist.

**Fix:**
Check the image repository URL and ensure the image is available.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'NodeNotReady'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the node is not in a ready state, possibly due to insufficient resources.

**Fix:**
Check the node's resource usage and ensure it meets the minimum requirements.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'CrashLoopBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that a pod is failing to start repeatedly, often due to misconfiguration.

**Fix:**
Check the pod logs with 'kubectl logs <pod-name>' to diagnose the issue.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for Kubernetes 1.30, improved Helm chart management.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated support for Kubernetes versions below 1.27.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from v2.13.x, ensure to update your Helm charts to the latest version.

resolution.summary: You should see a list of nodes with 'Ready' status when running 'kubectl get nodes'.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the necessary tools and resources before starting the installation.
